Title: Phytoparasite avoidance: Manipulation of strigolactone exudation, not biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling molecule that triggers parasitic weed seed germination; also involved in nutrient uptake and stress responses
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-binding cassette subtype G (ABCG) transporter; conserved phenylalanine in flexible loop of substrate release region
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-regulated by low-phosphate stress, strigolactone biosynthesis, and strigolactone signaling; expressed in roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone exudation from roots to rhizosphere; loss of function suppresses parasitic seed germination and helps maintain growth and yield in Striga-infested fields
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbSLT1 encodes a sorghum ABCG transporter required for root strigolactone exudation. 2. Double knockout with SbSLT2 strongly reduces strigolactone exudation and suppresses Striga seed germination. 3. This manipulation preserves crop growth and yield better than disrupting strigolactone biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transport-deficient genotypes are suggested to have less impact on plant growth and development than strigolactone-biosynthetic mutants; specific tradeoff for SbSLT1/2 double mutant not emphasized beyond possible symbiosis considerations in strigolactone transport manipulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AlphaFold2 and mutagenesis indicated SbSLT1 F693 is a conserved core residue for transport efficiency; homologous SbSLT1-LIKE lacking this site lost transport activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated disruption is a promising strategy to breed Striga-resistant sorghum with reduced yield penalty compared with blocking biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double knockout mutants; CRISPR-Cas9; site-directed mutagenesis; AlphaFold2-supported structural analysis; field phenotyping; strigolactone exudation assays

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling molecule that triggers parasitic weed seed germination; also involved in nutrient uptake and stress responses
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-binding cassette subtype G (ABCG) transporter; conserved phenylalanine in flexible loop of substrate release region
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-regulated by low-phosphate stress, strigolactone biosynthesis, and strigolactone signaling; expressed in roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone exudation from roots to rhizosphere; loss of function with SbSLT1 suppresses parasitic seed germination and helps maintain growth and yield in Striga-infested fields
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbSLT2 encodes a sorghum ABCG transporter involved in root strigolactone exudation. 2. Double knockout with SbSLT1 drastically reduces exuded strigolactone and Striga germination. 3. Structural evidence identifies F642 as critical for transport efficiency.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transport-deficient genotypes are suggested to have less impact on plant growth and development than strigolactone-biosynthetic mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SbSLT2 F642 is a conserved phenylalanine core residue in the transport channel; SbSLT2-LIKE lacking the site lost transport activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering Striga-avoidant sorghum by reducing root strigolactone exudation rather than biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double knockout mutants; CRISPR-Cas9; site-directed mutagenesis; AlphaFold2-supported structural analysis; field phenotyping; strigolactone exudation assays

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling molecule promoting parasitic weed seed germination; involved in long-distance signaling and nutrient-related adaptation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; weakly responsive to phosphate deficiency
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; weak response to Pi deficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates both root-to-rhizosphere strigolactone exudation and long-distance transport from roots to aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlABCG44 is a tomato ABCG transporter mediating strigolactone exudation and root-to-shoot transport. 2. It shares a conserved transport mechanism with sorghum SL transporters. 3. It contributes to parasitic weed germination by enabling host strigolactone release.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SL transport modulation may affect endogenous hormone distribution and symbiotic interactions, though knockout phenotype highlighted mainly for SlABCG45
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cross-group conservation with monocot and dicot SL transporters was noted; homologous to sorghum transporters in mechanism
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing broomrape stimulation via manipulation of strigolactone exudation/transport
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout-related transporter studies cited in tomato; transport phenotyping; expression response analysis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling molecule promoting parasitic weed seed germination; contributes to nutrient-stress adaptation and symbiotic signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in roots by phosphate deficiency
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; strongly induced by Pi deficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root strigolactone exudation and acropetal transport; knockout dramatically reduces exudation, limits parasitic emergence, and increases fruit number, total fruit weight, and yield under broomrape infestation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlABCG45 is a major tomato SL transporter for root exudation and root-to-shoot transport. 2. Its knockout markedly decreases strigolactone exudation and improves resistance to broomrape species. 3. Yield is improved under phytoparasite stress, but AMF marker gene expression decreases, indicating a symbiosis tradeoff.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slightly increased shoot branching; impaired root-to-shoot SL transport; decreased expression of AMF marker genes suggesting reduced mycorrhizal symbiosis establishment, especially relevant in Pi-deficient soils
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts within a positive feedback network coupling strigolactone biosynthesis and exudation; highlighted as a preferred target over biosynthesis genes because transporter knockout causes smaller developmental penalties
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding phytoparasite-avoiding tomato with improved yield resilience in infested fields, while monitoring impacts on AMF symbiosis and nutrient uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single knockout mutant analysis; field phenotyping under parasite infestation; strigolactone exudation and transport assays; AMF colonization/marker analysis; promoter binding evidence by upstream regulators

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Triggers parasitic seed germination and contributes to nutrient-stress adaptation/symbiotic signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone exudation indirectly by activating SlABCG45 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlNSP1 directly binds the SlABCG45 promoter. 2. It activates transporter expression that supports strigolactone exudation. 3. It participates in a positive feedback loop coupling strigolactone biosynthesis and exudation in tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop coupling strigolactone biosynthesis and exudation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator connecting nutrient-stress signaling to strigolactone transporter expression and exudation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with SlNSP2 in NSP1/2 regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SlABCG45 promoter (native, inducible by nutrient status context)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in the context of direct promoter binding and regulatory coupling rather than as the main experimental subject of this commentary article
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory target to tune transporter expression, but likely less straightforward than direct transporter editing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Direct promoter binding evidence reported; transcriptional regulation study cited from tomato literature

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Triggers parasitic seed germination and contributes to nutrient-stress adaptation/symbiotic signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone exudation indirectly by activating SlABCG45 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlNSP2 directly binds the SlABCG45 promoter. 2. It drives expression of a key strigolactone transporter in tomato roots. 3. It contributes to a positive feedback loop coordinating strigolactone biosynthesis and exudation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop coupling strigolactone biosynthesis and exudation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator linking nutrient status to enhanced SlABCG45-mediated strigolactone export
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with SlNSP1 in NSP1/2 regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SlABCG45 promoter (native, inducible by nutrient status context)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as part of the upstream regulation of SL exudation in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene for fine-tuning strigolactone exudation regulatory networks
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Direct promoter binding evidence reported; transcriptional regulation study cited from tomato literature
